title: Functionalization of kaolinite for removal of phosphate from urban sewage
author: Carbinatti, da Conceição, et al.

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: For validation purposes, 1.0 g of each sample (CK and FK) was mixed with 10 mL of aqueous solution containing PO43− in the initial concentration (C0) of 1 mg/L at pH 6 [9]. The suspension was shaken for 24 h at 145 rpm, and then centrifuged at 3000 rpm for 25 min. The supernatant was separated and the PO43- concentration remaining in solution (Ce) was measured using a Hach DR-2800 spectrophotometer, with a detection limit of 0.1 mg/L. The removal efficiency of PO43- (%A – in percentage) was determined according to the Eq. 1. The experimental procedures were carried out in triplicate.(1)%A=[(C0−−Ce)/Ce].100

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: The results are presented in Table 1. The removal efficiencies of PO43− were 0 and 100% using CK and FK, respectively. These results evidenced the functionalization plays a crucial role on the removal of PO43−. The precipitation of pyromorphite was the main mechanism associated with PO43- removal using FK with Pb(II), as shown in Fig. 1.Table 1Removal efficiency (%A) of PO43− (mg/L) using FK and CK in aqueous solution.Table 1CKFKC0Ce%AC0Ce%A1.01.0 ± 0.101.0< 0.1100Fig. 1SEM-EDS images of functionalized kaolinite after the removal of PO43−, showing the precipitation of small grains (lower than 5 µm) of pyromorphite (spots 1, 2 and 3).Fig. 1
